title: guettarda coatzacoalcensis
intro: Guettarda coatzacoalcensis, also known as the wild jasmine, is a flowering plant native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Guettarda coatzacoalcensis include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
